Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to write a DuckDB SQL query that filters out cases that have a specific three-event sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order in direct succession and where the time between Create Order and Confirm Order in that sequence is more than 5 days Then return all events from cases that are NOT filtered out

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interpretation Direct succession means no other events in between for that case So we need to find cases where these three activities occur consecutively in order Then among those check if the duration between the second and third Create Order to Confirm Order exceeds 5 days Then exclude those cases entirely We need to return all events from all other cases The query should use DuckDB syntax

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Approach Use window functions to identify the sequence For each case order events by timestamp Use LAG to get previous activity and next activity or use LEAD But we need to find triples Since direct succession we can check if an event is Create Order and its previous event is Approve Purchase Requisition and its next event is Confirm Order That gives us the middle event Then we can compute the time difference between that Create Order and its following Confirm Order If more than 5 days then that case is flagged

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Alternatively we can use array_agg or listagg to form a sequence and search for pattern but thats more complex Window functions are straightforward

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps 1 For each case order by timestamp 2 Use LAG activity1 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp as prev_activity 3 Use LEAD activity1 OVER PARTITION BY case_id ORDER BY timestamp as next_activity 4 Also use LEAD timestamp1 to get next timestamp 5 Filter rows where activity Create Order AND prev_activity Approve Purchase Requisition AND next_activity Confirm Order 6 Compute duration next_timestamp timestamp should be days In DuckDB timestamp subtraction gives an interval To get days use EXTRACT day from interval or cast to days Simpler next_timestamp DATE timestamp DATE 5 But careful if time difference includes hours we need more than 5 days ie more than 5 24 hours Using date difference might round down Better to use interval comparison next_timestamp timestamp INTERVAL 5 days DuckDB supports interval literals 7 Then get distinct case_ids that satisfy this condition 8 Then select all events from event_log where case_id NOT IN those case_ids

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Edge cases There could be multiple sequences in same case The condition says contains the following sequence so if any such sequence exists the whole case is excluded So we just need to detect at least one occurrence Also note The sequence must be in direct succession Our window approach checks consecutive events But what if the same case has multiple events We need to ensure that the Approve Purchase Requisition is immediately before and Confirm Order immediately after Thats correct But what if the sequence appears multiple times Still case is flagged

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Implementation Use CTE to get flagged case_ids Potential performance Use indexes Not needed Now write the final SQL Well include comments Note DuckDBs SQL dialect standard Use WITH clause Lets produce answer
